Defines static assertions for unsupported functions in Pebble firmware to provide clear error messages.

# Purpose
This C header file defines a set of macros to provide clear error messages when a developer attempts to use certain functions that are supported by `newlib-c` but not provided by the Pebble libraries. The file uses `_Static_assert` to generate compile-time errors for unsupported functions, such as file I/O operations (`fopen`, `fclose`, `fread`, etc.), print functions (`fprintf`, `sprintf`, etc.), general I/O functions (`open`, `close`, `read`, etc.), and memory management functions (`alloca`, `mmap`, etc.). Additionally, the file redefines the `printf` function to map to `app_log` for simple cross-compilation, ensuring that logging is consistent with the Pebble environment. This approach helps developers identify unsupported function calls early in the development process.
